component.name: RufusArm64
component.summary: Create bootable USB drives on Ubuntu ARM64

description.p: RufusArm64 is an unofficial bootable-USB creator designed for Ubuntu on ARM64 computers.

It writes raw and ISOHybrid media, prepares compressed and common virtual-disk inputs, creates Windows installation media, verifies signed acquisition catalogs, analyzes UEFI and Secure Boot metadata, and provides guarded persistent-live creation with optional ARM64 boot-time media-integrity validation.
Fixes packaged GTK startup on desktops that provide both GTK 3 and GTK 4 introspection by pinning GTK 3 before integrated dialog imports.
Adds the guarded graphical drive-image backup workflow and closes audited privilege, progress-channel, process-reaping, and report-validation edge cases before the Stage 1 release.

Adds descriptor-safe UEFI, DBX, and SBAT analysis plus an opt-in, reproducibly built ARM64 boot-time media-integrity validator for supported persistent Ubuntu/Debian writable-copy media, qualified under pinned AArch64 QEMU firmware and explicitly unsigned.
Prevents desktop automount races during persistent-live USB creation by setting and verifying the GPT do-not-automount attribute before FAT32 and ext4 formatting.
Completes the pre-parity safety and correctness audit with stronger identity, arithmetic, GPT durability, acquisition, GUI concurrency, static-analysis, Debian-policy, and reproducible-package gates.
Fixes persistence analysis for the official Ubuntu 26.04 ARM64 desktop image's harmless root-self alias while preserving strict escape, nested-root, and cycle rejection.
Consolidates the desktop experience to one visible RufusArm64 application entry with a Create Persistent Live USB action, while retaining the separate guarded persistence helper internally.
Fixes persistent-live analysis and verified FAT32 copying for bounded in-tree directory links such as Canonical images' dists/stable metadata path, while retaining escape and cycle rejection.
Aligns persistence analysis with the fresh GPT/FAT32/ext4 creator layout, fixes Canonical Resolute concept-image compatibility, and removes reliance on embedded ISOHybrid MBR geometry.

Adds the guarded graphical persistent-live USB wizard, identity-bound read-only analysis and creation, modern Ubuntu casper cmdline support, reboot qualification records, and the threshold-signed built-in acquisition-channel foundation.
Adds signed image acquisition, richer diagnostics, and a guarded CLI-only experimental Linux persistence workflow with verified GPT, FAT32, ext4, boot-file, and media-copy operations.
Adds compressed and virtual-disk preparation, Microsoft DBX updates, PE/COFF revocation checks, and target-sized expansion limits.
Adds true x86/x86-64 Windows BIOS/CSM media, Windows PE driver auto-loading, source hashing, strict symlink-safe driver copies, and pinned legacy boot-code verification.
Adds manual FAT32/NTFS selection, verified Rufus UEFI:NTFS media, NTFS checks, and optional Windows driver-folder staging.
Adds GPT/MBR UEFI selection, FAT32 cluster-size controls, full formatting, a one-pass media check, and clearer From-image behavior.
Responsive graphical ARM64 release with Windows Setup options, compact WIM progress, direct partition detection, a bundled WIM engine, and expanded safety tests.
